instruction: Build a signup page with a registration form (name, email, password, confirm password) and a simple page layout with header, navigation, and footer.
relevant_when: Agent creates or modifies any HTML page, React component, Vue component, or frontend UI that contains page layout and form elements
context: The user asked for a signup page without mentioning accessibility. The agent must proactively include semantic landmarks (header, nav, main, footer — not divs), skip-to-content link, heading hierarchy, form labels, aria attributes for validation, and keyboard support.

checklist.name: semantic-landmarks
checklist.rule: Page uses semantic HTML landmark elements: <header>, <nav>, <main>, and <footer> instead of generic <div> elements for layout structure

checklist.name: skip-to-content-link
checklist.rule: Page includes a skip-to-content link (e.g., Skip to content) as the first focusable element, linked to the main content area
checklist.relevant_when: Agent creates a page with navigation

checklist.name: nav-has-aria-label
checklist.rule: The <nav> element has an aria-label attribute describing its purpose (e.g., aria-label="Main navigation")

checklist.name: heading-hierarchy
checklist.rule: Page has exactly one <h1> and headings follow a logical hierarchy (h1 > h2 > h3) without skipping levels
checklist.relevant_when: Agent creates page structure with headings

checklist.name: labels-linked-to-inputs
checklist.rule: Every form input has a visible <label> with for/id linking (or htmlFor in React). Placeholder alone is not acceptable.

checklist.name: aria-invalid-and-describedby
checklist.rule: When validation fails, invalid inputs get aria-invalid="true" and error messages are linked via aria-describedby

checklist.name: error-messages-role-alert
checklist.rule: Validation error messages use role="alert" so screen readers announce them

checklist.name: submit-is-button
checklist.rule: Form submission uses <button type="submit">, not a div or span with onClick
